title: ferray-fft: complex FFTs only support Complex<f64> — no f32 FFT
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

All FFT functions in ferray-fft only accept Array<Complex<f64>, D> or Array<f64, D>. There's no support for Complex<f32> or f32 inputs. NumPy's FFT functions accept any numeric type and promote internally. The plan cache and rustfft both support f32 (FftPlanner<f32>), but ferray-fft hardcodes f64 everywhere. For ML workloads where data is f32, this forces an unnecessary f32->f64->f32 round-trip. Add f32 support either via generics over a trait (like LinalgFloat) or via separate f32 entry points.
